demos.text: In general, we advise you to try and keep the topology (the layout) of your DAG tasks relatively stable; dynamic DAGs are usually better used for dynamically loading configuration options or changing operator options.
demos.reasoning: The passage has the concise, authoritative style of technical documentation and presents a specific recommendation about DAG stability without the formulaic elaboration often associated with generated text. It is more likely human-authored.

demos.text: In general, try to keep the topology (that is, the layout) of your DAG tasks reasonably stable; dynamic DAGs tend to work better for loading configuration values dynamically or adjusting operator options as needed.
demos.reasoning: The passage has a polished, generalized instructional tone and uses a parenthetical clarification, which are common characteristics of AI-generated technical prose. Its wording is somewhat formulaic and lacks specific context or examples.
